message:
fix(selection): close TOCTOU race in duplicate-project-on-worker guard

The previous implementation had a time-of-check-to-time-of-use race:
has_active_build_for_project_on_worker() checked outside the lock, then
start_active_build() inserted under the lock, so two concurrent requests
for the same project could both pass the check and land on the same
worker.

Replace the two-step check-then-insert with try_start_active_build(),
which performs the duplicate check atomically under the write lock and
returns None on conflict. The caller now releases the reserved slots and
retries with the conflicting worker excluded.

Additionally, seed the excluded_worker_ids set up front via the new
active_workers_for_project() helper so the selector avoids workers that
already have an active build for the project before even attempting
reservation — reducing wasted retry cycles.

Includes unit tests for both new BuildHistory methods.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
